title: :Z is not always recursive for containers in pods
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**

/kind bug

**Description**

When bind mounting a directory with `:Z`/`:z` in a container which is run in a pod, podman doesn't always relabel the volume's contents recursively. It only does so if the directory itself already has the wrong label. However, if the directory already has the correct label (i.e. `container_file_t`), but some of the contents have the wrong label (i.e. `user_home_t`), the contents will not be relabeled to `container_file_t` by podman.

I'm actually not sure if this is expected behavior or not. I found https://github.com/opencontainers/selinux/pull/177 which appears to be related, but I'm not entirely sure. In any case, the behavior described above is surprising to me, especially since several manuals and articles on the 'net say that the relabeling happens recursively.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```sh
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# 1. Create dir and dir/file
mkdir dir && touch dir/file

# 2. Check their contexts
ls -1dZ dir dir/file
# unconfined_u:object_r:user_home_t:s0 dir
# unconfined_u:object_r:user_home_t:s0 dir/file

# 3. Create & start a pod
podman pod create --replace repro-pod
podman pod start repro-pod

# 4. Run a container in that pod which bind mounts `dir`
podman run \
  --rm \
  --pod=repro-pod \
  -v ./dir:/dir:ro,Z \
  registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal

# 5. Check context again
ls -1dZ dir dir/file
# system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c846,c1006 dir
# system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c846,c1006 dir/file

# 6. Restore context of `dir/file` (not of `dir`!)
restorecon -vF dir/file
# Relabeled /var/home/runiq/dir/file
# from system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c846,c1006
# to unconfined_u:object_r:user_home_t:s0

# 7. Create another container in that pod which mounts  `dir`
podman run \
  --rm \
  --pod=repro-pod \
  -v ./dir:/dir:ro,Z \
  registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal

# 8. Check context of `dir/file` again, it hasn't been relabeled correctly
ls -1dZ dir dir/file
# system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c846,c1006 dir
#            unconfined_u:object_r:user_home_t:s0 dir/file
```

**Describe the results you received:**

`dir/file` has not been relabeled to `container_file_t`

**Describe the results you expected:**

`dir/file` should have been relabeled to `container_file_t`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

- Happens regardless of image used
- Happens with rootful and rootless containers
- Happens with both `:z` and `:Z`
- Does *not* happen when the container is *not* in a pod
- Does *not* happen when *both* `dir` and `dir/file` have their labels restored before recreating the container (which is why I'm wondering whether this is intentional or not, and why I found https://github.com/opencontainers/selinux/pull/177)

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked [the Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)?**

Yes to both.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Fedora 37 Kinoite, physical host.

comments.body:
see https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14786, the idea was to improve performance since relabelling every time is slow when you have volumes with many files.
cc @rhatdan @giuseppe

Ah, so it is intentional. Good to know, thank you! :) I'll close this issue then.

Maybe that bit of info should be in the docs, though?
Care to open a PR to fix the man page?
